title: SCAN-PLAN.json
description: Output of sec-detect: detected languages/frameworks, capability→tool map, degraded categories, ai_pass trigger, and applicable reference appendices. Consumed by sec-vuln-scan / sec-triage. Derived from the detect_tools.py emitter (T-2.2); kept locked by test_scan_plan_schema.py.

capability.description: A security capability (the durable interface), never a brand.

category_tool.description: capability -> chosen tool name, or null when degraded to the floor.

degraded.description: Capabilities with no installed tool (fell back to Read/Grep/Glob).

kernel_adjacency.description: ADVISORY trust-boundary markers (kernel-adjacent / privileged-container code present). Informational only — drives an agent advisory note, NOT a finding (no CVSS, never a VULN-FINDINGS.json entry). Optional, so legacy SCAN-PLAN artifacts without it still validate.
